name: Los Puentes Medievales de Canencia y el río Lozoya — Sierra de Guadarrama
description: Explore the Sierra de Guadarrama on this moderate 24.1-mile mountain biking route, featuring medieval bridges and the Lozoya River.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Garganta De Los Montes are situated in the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, offering access to an extensive network of routes through mountains and forests. The region is characterized by the picturesque Valle del Lozoya, with the Lozoya River and Riosequillo reservoir contributing to the scenic beauty. Diverse landscapes include oak and pine forests, higher altitude meadows, and challenging mountain passes like Canencia Pass and Navafría Pass. Many routes follow historical paths, providing varied elevation and terrain for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Garganta De Los Montes The most popular mountain bike trail is Los Puentes Medievales de Canencia y el río Lozoya — Sierra de Guadarrama, a 24.1 miles (38.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route incorporates medieval bridges and segments alongside the Lozoya River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Canto Bridge of Canencia – Puente del Perdón loop from Canencia, a moderate 34.5 miles (55.5 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain through the Sierra de Guadarrama. Local mountain bikers also love the Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail leading through the scenic Lozoya River valley, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Garganta De Los Montes is defined by mountainous terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama, dense forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate trails to more challenging routes over mountain passes. The routes in Garganta De Los Montes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2,500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Garganta De Los Montes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Garganta De Los Montes?
A: There are over 160 mountain bike trails around Garganta De Los Montes, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, including oak and pine forests, make spring and autumn particularly pleasant for mountain biking, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on routes that offer shade through dense forests or higher altitudes.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: Yes, there are 21 easy mountain bike trails around Garganta De Los Montes. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 53 difficult routes. These often include significant elevation gains, such as those found on mountain passes like Canencia Pass and Navafría Pass, and more technical terrain within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly categorized, many of the easier and moderate trails can be suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. Look for routes with less elevation and smoother paths, often found alongside the Lozoya River or through less rugged forest areas.

Q: Can I find loop trails for mountain biking in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33849234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canto Bridge of Canencia – Puente del Perdón loop from Canencia&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain through the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: What scenic views can I expect on mountain bike trails around Garganta De Los Montes?
A: The trails offer spectacular views of the Valle del Lozoya, the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, and various reservoirs. You can enjoy vistas from points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, or ride alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojonavalle Forest&lt;/a&gt; also provides panoramic views of the Canencia valley.

Q: Can I see any historical sites while mountain biking in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: Many trails follow ancient paths, some centuries old, that were used to connect villages or move livestock. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345033484/los-puentes-medievales-de-canencia-y-el-rio-lozoya-sierra-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Puentes Medievales de Canencia y el río Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; incorporate medieval bridges and segments alongside the Lozoya River, offering a glimpse into the region's history.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama is rich in wildlife. While mountain biking, you might spot deer and wild boars, and a variety of bird species including griffon vultures, golden eagles, and peregrine falcons, especially in the more secluded forest areas.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages surrounding the trailheads, such as Garganta de los Montes, Canencia, or Buitrago del Lozoya. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas before your ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Garganta De Los Montes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the scenic beauty of the Valle del Lozoya, and the extensive network of trails that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes offer views of or pass by the region's reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19847408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matafrailes Bridge – Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint loop from El Cuadrón&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the Pinilla Reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Lozoya River – El Atazar Reservoir loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt; explores the area around the El Atazar Reservoir.
tips:
There is an easy single track from/to Canencia.
I managed it with a gravel (40mm tires)

The smallest and worst maintained of the three medieval bridges in Canencia, it is presented over the homonymous stream with a single arch and narrow stones placed like bricks, accompanied by an old mill rehabilitated as a home.
It is a very old bridge, Romanesque, over the Arroyo de Canencia, very close to the Town. The place is a recreation area with tables and seats to rest and talk.

This bridge built with its pillars over the Lozoya River gorge itself, obtained its side walls shortly after the Civil War. Its origins are uncertain. They could be Roman, the pass being located on a transit route on the way to Segovia. However, the area in which it is located is mentioned in the hunting book of Alfonso XI, medieval king of Castile. What is certain is that the bridge had a repair in the 19th century. XIV.
Nice viewpoint with recreational rest area

Of the three medieval bridges in Canencia, this is the furthest from the town, near the municipality of Gargantilla de Lozoya. Crossing the waters of the Canencia stream, this bridge of medieval origin has a single pointed arch, with a deck that is wider at the ends than in the center. Thanks to the height of the two rocks that function as its foundations, the height of the pass protected it from possible floods.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is approximately 38.8 km long with an elevation gain of about 640 meters. It typically takes around 3.5 hours to complete.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered moderate for mountain biking. While it is 100% cyclable, meaning you won't need to dismount for unrideable sections, good fitness and some advanced riding skills will enhance your experience, especially on parts with small stones and roots.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route. For a more enjoyable descent, it is recommended to ride it counter-clockwise.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain, including natural paths, wider tracks, and sections with riverbank vegetation. Expect areas with small stones and roots, especially around the medieval bridges. The path traverses beautiful valleys, meadows, and oak groves, with some parts featuring granite rocks and natural narrowings.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is famous for its well-preserved medieval bridges. You'll cross the historic Puente de las Cadenas, the Canto Bridge of Canencia, and the Puente Congosto over the Lozoya River, among others like the Matafrailes Bridge. There's also a scenic View of Lozoya Reservoir and the El Colladillo Pass.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning with vibrant foliage. Spring is also excellent with pleasant temperatures. To avoid extreme heat, summer mornings are best, and winter can be ridden, but be prepared for colder conditions and potential snow at higher elevations in the Sierra de Guadarrama.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this trail. However, please ensure your dog is kept under control, preferably on a leash, to respect wildlife, other trail users, and grazing cattle in the meadows.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain bike trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed, but the route is accessible from the vicinity of Canencia. Look for parking options in or near the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails, including the Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 2, Entorno al Cerro de la Cruz, and the Camino Natural del Valle del Lozoya. It also briefly touches parts of Pueblos del Valle de Lozoya and Centro BTT "Valle del Lozoya" - Ruta 3.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, the nearby village of Canencia and other towns in the Lozoya Valley offer cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel before or after your ride.

waypoints.name: Puente Congosto over the Lozoya River
